summary: Round 3 did produce a real authority-backed same-schema improvement: Rust HNSW moved from 521.0310353032352 to 553.060494112464 qps (+6.1%) while recall rose from 0.9922999999999992 to 0.9942999999999994, and the remote synthetic profile also got cheaper. Native HNSW, however, also rose to 10792.646 qps, so the native-over-Rust gap only narrowed from 20.2x to 19.5x. That is enough to reject a hard-stop on the distance-compute hypothesis, but not enough to justify rewriting the historical HNSW family verdict or reopening leadership claims.
action: Keep benchmark_results/hnsw_p3_002_final_verdict.json unchanged, archive round 3 as a soft-stop on the current authority evidence, and only reopen HNSW again with a new authority-backed hypothesis or a materially stronger same-schema gain.
